title: Thecacoris bussei

This family is part of the larger order Malpighiales within the dicotyledonous angiosperms. The plant has been traditionally used in African folk medicine, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, which have been found to aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, wounds, and respiratory infections.
